easy.companyDescription: A specialized chip designer whose high-performance hardware serves enthusiasts and commercial computing buyers.
easy.macroContext: Cloud platforms and data-heavy workloads are increasing demand for specialized processing power.
easy.situation: Can a niche hardware category expand into larger commercial workloads without being overpriced as a cycle?
easy.longCase: New commercial workloads could expand demand beyond the original enthusiast market.
easy.shortCase: A cyclical chip market and volatile expectations make early growth easy to overprice.
setupHints: The company already holds a strong niche in performance-focused computing hardware.

medium.companyDescription: A technology component supplier with several types of customers.
medium.macroContext: Large business buyers are adjusting procurement as equipment budgets shift across categories.
medium.situation: Is stronger demand from a changing customer mix durable enough to support current expectations?
medium.longCase: More spending from additional customer groups could support revenue beyond the historical base.
medium.shortCase: Pricing pressure and uneven equipment budgets could reverse an early improvement in demand.

hard.macroContext: Business buyers are reallocating equipment budgets while suppliers compete for a changing mix of orders.
hard.situation: Can a broader order base support earnings if purchasing patterns remain uneven?
hard.longCase: Orders from more customer groups could reduce reliance on any one source of demand.
hard.shortCase: Competitive pricing and delayed purchases could weaken results if budgets become more cautious.

reveal.shortText: That was Nvidia. Demand for its specialized chips broadened well beyond the original enthusiast market, and the stock more than tripled.
reveal.funFact: Hardware once aimed mainly at enthusiasts became central to training modern machine-learning models.
whyItMoved: Commercial and data-center demand for specialized processors rose.
Investors re-rated the company's addressable market.
Performance leadership supported premium pricing for a period.

sources.label: Prototype sample seed
sources.notes: Placeholder sample content for Phase 3 pipeline; not production research.

review.reviewNotes: H029 rewrote Medium and Hard after H028 connected a 2015 semiconductor designer, large-computing buyers, multi-end-market demand, and a durable-shift debate to Nvidia. Medium peer set: Nvidia/AMD/Intel/Broadcom. Hard peer set: Intel/AMD/Broadcom/Qualcomm/Micron/Nvidia; the decision core addresses generic order diversification and equipment-budget risk. Easy and shared labels remain unchanged; Medium/Hard Gate 2 results were removed as stale.

prohibited: semiconductor designer plus multi-end-market 2015 demand conjunction

direction.cue: Additional customer groups could make the demand improvement more durable than a single-cycle spike

guesses.pointingFact: 2015 computing infrastructure cycle for a multi-customer technology supplier

direction.cue: Broader orders could help, but cautious budgets and pricing pressure cut both ways
